notes: Iteration 5 patch: AC10 check object gains two flat keys — old_root_form_absent_verification_command and old_root_form_absent_exit_code: 1 — to verify that the old /root/.claude/ absolute invocation form is absent from commands/push.md after M13 is applied. AC10 when and then text updated accordingly. ac_uid bumped to v5. No other ACs changed. Codex-reviewed: nested-sub-object shape rejected in favour of flat keys; compound-semicolon expression rejected in favour of direct grep + expected exit_code: 1; path-only pattern used instead of python3-prefixed pattern to catch any absolute invocation form.
test_isolation_contract: All ACs that require a mock push.sh use HOME=<tmpdir> isolation. Mock push.sh at <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh. Script invoked by absolute path /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/execute-push.py. NEVER write to ~/.claude/hooks/push.sh. Mock uses MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH env var (not EXECUTE_PUSH_TEST_CAPTURE). Mock must NOT write to stderr.

acceptance_criteria.given: A valid push-analyst grant JSON at /tmp/agentic-commit/push-analyst/<repo-hash>/<session-id>/<request-id>.json with verdict=approved, all fields correct (nonce==REQUEST_ID, branch==BRANCH, remote_name==REMOTE, session_id==SESSION_ID, expires_at in the future using Z-suffix, head_sha equals current git HEAD); CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set; test harness sets HOME=<tmpdir> and creates mock push.sh (chmod 0755, valid shebang #!/bin/sh) at <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh that reads MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH env var and writes {"argv": "$1", "CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID": "$CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID"} as JSON to that path then exits 0; test harness sets MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH=/tmp/execute-push-capture-<pid>.json
acceptance_criteria.when: HOME=<tmpdir> python3 /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/execute-push.py --repo-hash <hash> --branch <branch> --remote <remote> --request-id <id> is invoked (absolute script path, HOME isolation for push.sh lookup)
acceptance_criteria.then: MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH file exists with CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID matching request-id AND argv containing the remote value; Chain-B sentinel exists at correct path with exactly the 5 keys {result, request_id, head, branch, remote} and result=PASS with all values matching; grant file is absent (consumed)

acceptance_criteria.given: A push-analyst grant where expires_at is in the past OR any non-head_sha mandatory field is wrong/missing/mismatched (e.g. nonce does not match request-id, branch does not match, session_id does not match); head_sha is set to current HEAD so step 9 would pass — only step 7 fires; test harness sets HOME=<tmpdir> and creates a valid mock push.sh at <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh so the script reaches grant validation (step 4 passes)
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 1; no Chain-B sentinel file exists at the sentinel path; stderr contains a descriptive message naming the failing field; grant file still exists at grant path (not consumed)

acceptance_criteria.given: A push-analyst grant where all fields EXCEPT head_sha are valid (nonce, branch, remote_name, session_id, expires_at, verdict=approved), but grant.head_sha is set to a SHA that is NOT the current git HEAD (e.g. a prior commit SHA); head_sha is deliberately different from current HEAD so step 9 fires; steps 1-8 all pass; test harness sets HOME=<tmpdir> and creates a valid mock push.sh at <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 1; no Chain-B sentinel file exists; stderr contains a message indicating head_sha mismatch or HEAD drift; grant file still exists at grant path (not consumed)

acceptance_criteria.given: A valid grant with verdict=blocked and a non-empty risks[] array; all other fields correct; head_sha equals current HEAD; test harness sets HOME=<tmpdir> and creates a valid mock push.sh at <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 1; no Chain-B sentinel file exists; stderr displays the risks[] contents; grant file still exists at grant path (blocked grant intentionally not consumed)

acceptance_criteria.given: A valid grant with verdict=warn and a non-empty risks[] array; all other fields correct; head_sha equals current HEAD; CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set; test harness sets HOME=<tmpdir> and creates mock push.sh at <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh (chmod 0755, must NOT write to stderr so captured stderr reflects only execute-push.py output); test harness sets MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH=/tmp/execute-push-capture-<pid>.json
acceptance_criteria.when: HOME=<tmpdir> python3 /dev/shm/dev-workspace/dot-claude/scripts/execute-push.py --repo-hash <hash> --branch <branch> --remote <remote> --request-id <id> is invoked via subprocess.run(stderr=subprocess.PIPE) to capture stderr
acceptance_criteria.then: Mock push.sh is exec'd (MOCK_CAPTURE_PATH file exists with CLAUDE_PUSH_REQUEST_ID matching request-id); Chain-B sentinel exists with result=PASS; grant file is absent (consumed); captured stderr bytes contain each element of risks[] from the grant

check.note: Invoke via subprocess.run(stderr=subprocess.PIPE); mock push.sh must not write to stderr

acceptance_criteria.given: All grant conditions valid (approved verdict, correct fields, future expiry, head_sha == current HEAD); CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set; test harness sets HOME=<tmpdir> where <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh does NOT exist
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 2 (infrastructure failure); no Chain-B sentinel file exists (abort at step 4, before sentinel write); grant file still exists at grant path (not consumed; abort before step 10); stderr mentions push.sh (missing or not executable)

acceptance_criteria.given: All grant conditions valid (approved verdict, correct fields, future expiry, head_sha == current HEAD); CLAUDE_SESSION_ID set; test harness sets HOME=<tmpdir> and creates a mock push.sh at <tmpdir>/.claude/hooks/push.sh (chmod 0755) with a shebang pointing to a path the harness has asserted does not exist (e.g. #!/tmp/execute-push-nointerp-<pid> where that path does not exist), causing os.execv to raise OSError
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is 2 (infrastructure failure — M11 sentinel cleanup path); Chain-B sentinel file does NOT exist at the sentinel path (M11 handler unlinked it); grant file is absent (was consumed at step 10 before sentinel write)

check.note: M11: execv OSError handler must unlink sentinel before returning exit 2; grant consumed before sentinel write so grant IS absent

acceptance_criteria.given: CLAUDE_SESSION_ID is unset or empty in the environment; all other arguments otherwise valid
acceptance_criteria.then: Exit code is exactly 2 (not 1); stderr contains 'CLAUDE_SESSION_ID'; no grant file is read (script aborts before grant path is accessed); no sentinel file is written

acceptance_criteria.given: commands/push.md has been updated by the dev cycle (M12 applied)
acceptance_criteria.when: The Step 4 section of commands/push.md is examined via grep
acceptance_criteria.then: grep -i 'MUST NOT' commands/push.md returns at least one match near Step 4 heading (confirming orchestrator must not manually validate or unlink); awk '/```/{in_code=!in_code} in_code{print}' commands/push.md | grep -E 'os\.unlink\(|rm -f .*grant' exits code 1 (no manual unlink/rm-grant instruction inside any code fence); grep 'execute-push.py' commands/push.md returns output confirming script is referenced

acceptance_criteria.given: commands/push.md has been updated by the dev cycle (M13 applied)
acceptance_criteria.when: grep 'execute-push.py' commands/push.md is run, grep -E '&&.*push\.sh|validate_sentinel\.py|bash[[:space:]].*push\.sh' commands/push.md is run, and grep -F '/root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md is run
acceptance_criteria.then: grep -F 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md returns at least one match (tilde form, NOT /root/.claude/); grep -A8 -F 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md output contains --branch AND --request-id AND --repo-hash AND --remote (continuation lines captured via -A8); grep -E '&&.*push\.sh|validate_sentinel\.py|bash[[:space:]].*push\.sh' commands/push.md exits with code 1 (no old && pattern, no validate_sentinel.py reference, no direct bash push.sh invocation); grep -F '/root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md exits with code 1 (old /root/.claude/ absolute execute-push path absent)

check.grep_check: grep -F 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md must return at least one match
